role: First Deputy Speaker of the Islamic Consultative Assembly (2004–2016); brother of PM Mohammad-Javad Bahonar
bio: Mohammad-Reza Bahonar is an Iranian conservative politician and the brother of assassinated Prime Minister [Mohammad-Javad Bahonar](/people/mohammad-javad-bahonar). He served as First Deputy Speaker of the [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) from 2004 to 2016, elected with 188 out of 259 votes. A prominent principlist parliamentarian during the [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad) era, he was also the uncle of Mojtaba Samareh Hashemi, a senior aide to Ahmadinejad. Bahonar was a member of the Society of Islamic Revolution's Devotees (Isargaran) and a fixture of conservative parliamentary politics for over two decades.
editorial_notes: Born 1952 in Kerman. Brother of PM Bahonar. First Deputy Speaker 2004-2016. Uncle of Mojtaba Samareh Hashemi.
